seoTitle: How to Do SEO on GoHighLevel Websites
metaDescription: A practical look at optimizing a GoHighLevel-built website for search, covering page settings, metadata, and the basics that help pages get found.
intro: Building a website in GoHighLevel is only half the job if it never shows up in search results. This guide covers the core SEO settings available inside GoHighLevel's website builder so your pages have a real chance of ranking.

steps.heading: Set page titles and meta descriptions
steps.body: Every page in your GoHighLevel site builder has fields for title tags and meta descriptions, which are the first things search engines and searchers see.

steps.heading: Use clear, keyword-relevant page names
steps.body: Make sure your page URLs and headings reflect what the page is actually about, rather than generic labels that don't tell search engines or visitors anything.

steps.heading: Optimize images and page speed
steps.body: Compress images and keep pages lean, since slower-loading pages tend to rank worse and lose visitors before they even see your content.

steps.heading: Add structured headings
steps.body: Organize your page content with a clear heading hierarchy so both readers and search engines can understand how the page is structured.

steps.heading: Submit your sitemap and monitor performance
steps.body: Once your site is live, submit it to search engines and keep an eye on how pages are performing so you can adjust based on what's actually working.

faq.question: Does GoHighLevel have built-in SEO tools?
faq.answer: Yes, the website builder includes fields for meta titles, descriptions, and other on-page settings you can adjust per page.

faq.question: Do I need a separate SEO plugin for a GoHighLevel site?
faq.answer: For basic on-page SEO, GoHighLevel's built-in settings are usually enough, though more advanced strategies may involve external tools for tracking and research.

faq.question: How long does it take to see SEO results on a new site?
faq.answer: SEO typically takes weeks to months to show measurable results, so it's worth treating it as an ongoing effort rather than a one-time setup.

faq.question: Can page speed really affect my rankings?
faq.answer: Yes, slow-loading pages tend to perform worse in search results and also lead to higher visitor drop-off, so keeping pages fast matters for both.
